headline: Nvidia to sell 1 million chips to Amazon by end of 2027 in cloud deal
summary: Nvidia will sell 1 million of its graphics processing unit chips, along with a host of ‌the AI giant's other offerings, to Amazon.com's cloud computing unit by 2027, ‌a Nvidia executive told Reuters on Thursday.  Nvidia and Amazon Web Services said this week that AWS had ​reached a deal to buy its 1 million GPUs but had not disclosed the precise timing of the deal.  Ian Buck, vice president of hyperscale and high-performance computing at Nvidia, told Reuters on Thursday that the sales would start this year and ‌extend through 2027.

Nvidia and Amazon Web Services said this week that AWS had ​reached a deal to buy its 1 million GPUs but had not disclosed the precise timing of the deal.  Ian Buck, vice president of hyperscale and high-performance computing at Nvidia, told Reuters on Thursday that the sales would start this year and ‌extend through 2027.

At scale, this represents more than $200 million in cumulative potential operational value across the network over a three-year period.

headline: US applications for jobless benefits fall to 205,000 last week as layoffs remain historically low.
summary: U.S. applications for unemployment benefits fell last week, remaining in the same range of recent years despite a broadly tepid labor market.  The number of Americans filing for jobless aid for the week ending March 14 fell by 8,000 from the previous week to 205,000, the Labor Department reported Thursday.  Filings for unemployment benefits are viewed as a proxy for U.S. layoffs and are close to a real-time indicator of the health of the job market.

Filings for unemployment benefits are viewed as a proxy for U.S. layoffs and are close to a real-time indicator of the health of the job market.

headline: Oracle’s ‘Di-SaaS-ter’ Leaves the Stock Too Cheap. Buy It.
summary: Shares of the software provider trade 52% below the record high they hit in late September.  Oracle not only has a legacy business that can survive artificial intelligence, but the company is integral to the AI revolution.  As a core supplier to the pioneering AI application developers—ChatGPT’s OpenAI and Claude’s Anthropic, among others—Oracle can sustain high sales growth, put its large capital expenditures behind it, and grow earnings aggressively.
